messages.content:
CVE-2024-21601 describes a race condition vulnerability in the Flow-processing Daemon (flowd) of Juniper Networks Junos OS on SRX Series devices. This vulnerability arises from concurrent execution using a shared resource with improper synchronization, specifically a queue used for TCP events processing. If exploited, it can cause a Denial-of-Service (DoS) condition, although exploitation is outside the direct control of attackers as one of the threads cannot be triggered externally.

This vulnerability is identified with CWE-362, indicating an improper synchronization issue. It's classified with a severity level of 'MEDIUM' and is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1498, which relates to network service disruptions.

Affected versions include certain releases of Junos OS 21.2 to 22.4, with mitigations such as updating to the latest versions as specified: 21.2R3-S5, 21.3R3-S5, 21.4R3-S4, 22.1R3-S3, 22.2R3-S1, 22.3R3, and 22.4R3. Implementing M1037 type mitigations is also recommended for reducing potential impacts.
